name: Horseradish Mustard
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup White wine vinegar (or Oriental rice vinegar)
6 Whole black peppercorns (crushed in a mortar) or a pinch of ground black pepper
2 Whole allspice berries (crushed in a mortar) or a small pinch of ground allspice
A little dry white wine (or additional white wine vinegar if needed)
recipeInstructions:
1. In a bowl, stir together the dry mustard and water. Let stand, uncovered, for 20 minutes, stirring once or twice.
2. In the container of a blender or food processor, combine the vinegar, salt, horseradish, garlic, sugar, peppercorns, and allspice. Process until the garlic and horseradish are pureed in the liquid. Strain through a fine-mesh strainer, pressing all juice from any pulp.
3. Combine the strained liquid with the mustard-water mixture in the top of a double boiler set over simmering water. Cook, stirring constantly, for about 5 minutes, or until the mustard has thickened (it will thicken more while cooling).
4. Cool and taste. Add more prepared horseradish if desired, and if the mustard seems too thick, thin it with a few drops of white wine or additional vinegar.
Makes about 1/2 cup
